title: niphobolus princeps
intro: Niphobolus princeps, also known as the Queen's crown fern, is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Niphobolus princeps are typically made by drying the fronds and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or added to salves and creams for topical application.
